title: achromolaena viscosa
intro: Achromolaena viscosa, also known as sticky leaf or forest tea, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Achromolaena viscosa include treating fever, malaria, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a natural antiseptic to prevent infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal use.
